I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. [{"review_id": "reviewid_135", "user_id": "userid_548", "business_ref": "businessref_34", "rating": "2", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.", "date": "August 01, 2016 at 03:44 AM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1067", "user_id": "userid_213", "business_ref": "businessref_89", "rating": "5", "useful": "2", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too", "date": "June 14, 2021 at 11:39 AM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_871", "user_id": "userid_616", "business_ref": "businessref_82", "rating": "4", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this \"burger\" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our \"server\" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!", "date": "29 May 2013, 23:01"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_314", "user_id": "userid_1903", "business_ref": "businessref_66", "rating": "2", "useful": "1", "funny": "2", "cool": "1", "text": "This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare", "date": "21 May 2016, 18:48"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_487", "user_id": "None", "business_ref": "businessref_95", "rating": "1", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened", "date": "November 01, 2021 at 05:11 PM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1031", "user_id": "userid_206", "business_ref": "businessref_24", "rating": "4", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "A cornucupia of flavors and toppings. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.", "date": "2013-07-08 21:47:00"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_441", "user_id": "userid_380", "business_ref": "businessref_40", "rating": "4", "useful": "0", "funny": "1", "cool": "0", "text": "You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.", "date": "March 02, 2009 at 09:43 PM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_40", "user_id": "None", "business_ref": "businessref_47", "rating": "5", "useful": "1", "funny": "0", "cool": "1", "text": "Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.", "date": "March 16, 2014 at 12:40 AM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1807", "user_id": "userid_1546", "business_ref": "businessref_16", "rating": "3", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.", "date": "September 18, 2014 at 05:50 PM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1741", "user_id": "userid_1348", "business_ref": "businessref_96", "rating": "5", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.", "date": "17 Apr 2017, 14:04"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_418", "user_id": "userid_546", "business_ref": "businessref_46", "rating": "5", "useful": "1", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "This is some quality BBQ! I found the food to be excellent and the staff to be kind/helpful. The brisket is so tasty, and the ribs were fall-off-the-bone good! The restaurant also uses this phone app called GiftAMeal, which let me donate 2 free meals to a food pantry just by snapping a pic of my food via the app. I thought that was really neat, on top of a great dining experience. I shall return.", "date": "2016-08-15 21:16:00"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1222", "user_id": "userid_254", "business_ref": "businessref_21", "rating": "1", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "I really wish i had checked out the Yelp reviews beforehand.  I've purchased about 10 used cars in the last 25 years. This has been the worst experience by far. Test drove and purchased a used car on 3/31 for my 16 year old daughter, the salesman knew it was for her. Bought the 3 year 'bumper to bumper' warranty the finance guy said was the same as if it \"just came off the showroom floor\". They needed to remove pin stripes and fix a tire pressure sensor so they agreed to deliver it; we live almost two hours away. Met them after dark near the highway on 4/2; the check engine light came on on our way home. Texted a pic to the salesman and called him the next day -- no response. Left several voice mails for the warranty/finance guy - no response. Took it to a friend at local dealer who hooked it up to the diagnostic machine and said it was the after-market high-flow air intake that caused the warning light. I would not have purchased the car had I known that. Then another warning light - reduced engine performance, reduce clutch use. To make a very long story short - clutch goes, daughter gets stranded on busy highway, vehicle towed back to Ford of Port Richey; lots of promises about fixing it and being at the front of the service line, zero follow up, 53 days of ownership, 31 days out of commission/in shop and the story ends with a bill for $2000, even though they ultimately acknowledged one of their managers drove the car for three days because the check engine light kept coming on before they sold it. I won't even mention the cracked windshield we saw the morning after they delivered - just turn it in on your insurance. I understand it is free but still counts as a claim.   I'm just glad 16 year old girls are patient and understanding.", "date": "08 Jun 2019, 07:10"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1928", "user_id": "userid_1151", "business_ref": "businessref_9", "rating": "5", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "I had a great experience at Coffee House Too. I came on a Sunday around brunch and there was no wait for a table, and the service was exceptional. As you wait for your food, you are offered a complimentary mimosa as well as a muffin. On top of that, the cost for food is relatively low compared to other brunch locations. I highly recommend this restaurant and will definitely be coming back!", "date": "2016-07-18 21:37:00"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1790", "user_id": "userid_668", "business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "1", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Breakfast is very expensive at $7 for a breakfast sandwich meal with oj. You could get the same deal at a sit down restaurant. The oj was warm so I asked for ice. The woman at the window informed me that if I wanted ice, I had to ask for it when I placed the order. At that time, I didn't know the oj was warm. I don't ever go to McDonald's but, if do again, I would go to the one right down the street near

{"code": "import json
import re
from collections import defaultdict

# Get all businesses that accept credit cards
credit_card_businesses = []
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:34']

print('Total businesses with credit cards:', len(businesses))

# Extract categories from business descriptions
category_counts = defaultdict(int)
business_ratings = defaultdict(list)

# Process each business that accepts credit cards
for business in businesses:
    business_id = business['business_id']
    description = business.get('description', '')
    
    # Extract categories from description (look for phrases after "in" or categories separated by commas)
    categories = []
    
    # Look for "in Education, Elementary Schools, ..." pattern
    if 'services in' in description:
        parts = description.split('services in')
        if len(parts) > 1:
            category_part = parts[1]
            # Remove location info if present
            if ', this' in category_part:
                category_part = category_part.split(', this')[0]
            # Split by commas and clean
            cats = [cat.strip() for cat in category_part.split(',')]
            categories.extend(cats)
    
    # Also look for "including Hair Salons, Beauty & Spas, ..." pattern
    if 'including' in description:
        parts = description.split('including')
        if len(parts) > 1:
            category_part = parts[1]
            if '.' in category_part:
                category_part = category_part.split('.')[0]
            cats = [cat.strip() for cat in category_part.split(',')]
            categories.extend(cats)
    
    # Clean up categories
    categories = [cat for cat in categories if cat and len(cat) > 2]
    
    if not categories:
        # If no clear categories found, use a generic one
        categories = ['General Business']
    
    print(f"Business: {business['name']}")
    print(f"  Categories: {categories}")
    
    # Store business ID for later rating lookup
    for cat in categories:
        category_counts[cat] += 1
        
print('\nCategory counts:', dict(category_counts))

# Now let's get ratings for these businesses from the review table
print('\nLooking up ratings...')"}

{"code": "import json, re, os
from collections import defaultdict

# Read business data
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:34']
print('Total credit card businesses:', len(businesses))

# Extract categories and map business IDs to categories
category_counts = defaultdict(int)
business_to_categories = {}

for business in businesses:
    business_id = business['business_id']
    desc = business.get('description', '')
    name = business.get('name', '')
    
    # Find categories in description
    categories = []
    if 'services in' in desc:
        part = desc.split('services in')[-1]
        if ', this' in part:
            part = part.split(', this')[0]
        cats = [c.strip() for c in part.split(',') if c.strip()]
        categories.extend(cats)
    
    if 'including' in desc:
        part = desc.split('including')[-1]
        if '.' in part:
            part = part.split('.')[0]
        cats = [c.strip() for c in part.split(',') if c.strip()]
        categories.extend(cats)
    
    if not categories:
        categories = ['General Business']
    
    business_to_categories[business_id] = categories
    for cat in categories:
        category_counts[cat] += 1

print('Top categories:', sorted(category_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:10])

# Read review data
review_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:24']
if isinstance(review_file, str) and os.path.exists(review_file):
    with open(review_file, 'r') as f:
        reviews = json.load(f)
    print('Total reviews:', len(reviews))
else:
    reviews = []
    print('No review file found')"}

message.reasoning:
The user wants to find which business category has the largest number of businesses that accept credit card payments, and what is its average rating.

Let me break down the task:
1. Identify businesses that accept credit card payments (from businessinfo_database)
2. Extract business categories from those businesses (from the description field)
3. Find ratings for those businesses (from user_database review table)
4. Calculate which category has the most credit-card-accepting businesses
5. Calculate the average rating for that category

From the data I've seen:
- The business collection in MongoDB has an attributes.BusinessAcceptsCreditCards field
- The description field contains category information like "providing a range of services in Education, Elementary Schools, Child Care & Day Care, Local Services, Preschools, and Montessori Schools."
- The review table in DuckDB has ratings with business_ref that corresponds to business_id (with different prefixes businessid_ vs businessref_)

Let me:
1. Get all businesses that accept credit cards
2. Extract categories from their descriptions
3. Get all reviews to map ratings to businesses
4. Join the data to calculate category counts and average ratings
5. Find the category with most businesses

I need to:
- Query businesses with BusinessAcceptsCreditCards = "True"
- Extract categories from descriptions
- Query all reviews
- Map business_id to business_ref (convert businessid_X to businessref_X)
- Calculate the results

Let me start fresh with a proper plan.

You are a data analysis agent. Use only the tools listed below to answer the user's query, based on the provided DATABASE DESCRIPTION for logical database names and their types (SQL or MongoDB), and the results of previous tool calls.

TOOLS (system will execute):
- query_db: run a SQL or Mongo query. Returns a list of JSON-serializable records or an error string.
  Required args: {"db_name": "<logical_db_name>", "query": "<SQL or Mongo query>"}
- list_db: list tables or collections for a given database.
  Required args: {"db_name": "<logical_db_name>"}
- execute_python: run Python code.
  Required args: {"code": "<python_code>"}
- return_answer: finish and return the final answer (plain text).
  Required args: {"answer": "<final plain-text answer>"}

INSTRUCTIONS:
1. After each tool call, its result will be stored in a storage under a key named after the tool call id (you will be told the key name). The next message will include both the result (or a preview if it's large) and the storage key name.
2. Inside execute_python code you may read storage entries using the provided key names, e.g., if the tool call id is 'functions.tool:id', you can access its result via `locals()['var_functions.tool:id']` in your code.
3. You cannot modify or reassign those storage-provided variables; you may read them and create new variables as needed.
4. If a tool result is large, the next message will include a preview (first 10000 characters) and the storage entry will be the .json file path (a string) where the full result is stored. To access the full result, your execute_python code must open and read that .json file.

KEY RULES (must follow exactly):
1. Always use tool calls. Do not output plain text, explanations, or reasoning.
2. Include all required arguments for the tool you call.
3. For query_db, always specify db_name and query. Refer the DATABASE DESCRIPTION for db_name and query format.
4. For PostgreSQL, wrap mixed-case or uppercase column names in double quotes.
5. For list_db, refer the DATABASE DESCRIPTION to specify db_name.
6. Use execute_python for data processing as needed. 
7. When using execute_python, your code will be quoted by triple double-quotes and passed as a string to `exec(...)` for execution in a Python 3.12 environment with only pandas and pyarrow installed. So you must ensure your code is compatible with this execution method. For exampe, do not use triple double-quotes in your code, as they may interfere with parsing. Do not use non-built-in or non-installed packages.
8. When using execute_python, your code must print the result at the end exactly as shown in the PRINT FORMAT section below. The printed result must be a string that can be successfully parsed by json.loads() without errors.

PRINT FORMAT (must match exactly):
----BEGIN PRINT FORMAT----
print("__RESULT__:")
print(your_json_serializable_string_here)
----END PRINT FORMAT----

For simple types (int, float, str, bool, None), you may use json.dumps() to produce a valid JSON string.
For complex or non-JSON-serializable types, you must convert them into JSON-compatible forms before printing.
For lists or dictionaries, you must ensure that all nested elements are also converted into JSON-serializable types.
9. Return the final answer only via a single return_answer tool call. Do not include extra text, explanation, or formatting.

EXAMPLES:
- query_db:
{"tool": "query_db", "args": {"db_name": "some_db_name", "query": "SELECT * FROM some_table LIMIT some_limit;"}} # for SQL databases
{"tool": "query_db", "args": {"db_name": "some_db_name", "query": "{"collection": "some_collection", "filter": {some_filter}, "projection": {some_projection}, "limit": some_limit}"}} # for MongoDB databases

- list_db:
{"tool": "list_db", "args": {"db_name": "some_db_name"}}

- execute_python:
{"tool": "execute_python", "args": {"code": "import pandas as pd
# rl1 and rl2 are the keys of two JSON-serializable record lists in storage
df1 = pd.DataFrame(rl1)
df2 = pd.DataFrame(rl2)
result = pd.merge(df1, df2, on='id').head(10).to_json(orient='records')
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)"}}

- return_answer:
{"tool": "return_answer", "args": {"answer": "…final plain-text answer…"}}

If you cannot proceed, call return_answer with a short explanatory message.

Do not output explanations, reasoning, or any natural language outside of the required tool calls.

QUERY:
Which business category has the largest number of businesses that accept credit card payments, and what is its average rating?

DATABASE DESCRIPTION:
You are working with two databases to solve this query.

Here are the descriptions of these two databases:

1. businessinfo_database
   - This database is stored in a MongoDB database and contains metadata and user check-in records for Yelp businesses. It captures business attributes, service descriptions, operating hours, and user visit timestamps.
   - This database consists of two collections:
     - business
       - This collection contains metadata for businesses listed on Yelp, including names, identifiers, operational status, attributes, business hours, and service/location descriptions.
       - Fields:
         - _id
         - business_id (str): Unique business identifier
         - name (str): Business name
         - review_count (int): Total number of reviews
         - is_open (int): Whether business is currently open (1=open, 0=closed)
         - attributes (dict or null): Business attributes (parking, WiFi, etc.)
         - hours (dict or null): Operating hours
         - description (str): Business description including location information

     - checkin
       - This collection records the check-in behavior of Yelp users at various businesses, capturing the timestamped log of user presence.
       - Fields:
         - _id
         - business_id (str): Business identifier linking to business collection
         - date (list of str): List of check-in timestamps

2. user_database
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains user profiles and user-generated content from Yelp, including written reviews, tips, and engagement statistics such as review counts, votes, and elite status.
   - This database consists of three tables:
     - review
       - This table contains user-generated reviews of businesses on Yelp, including rating scores, review content, and metadata such as user and business reference identifiers.
       - Fields:
         - review_id (str): Unique review identifier
         - user_id (str or null): User identifier
         - business_ref (str): Business identifier linking to the business collection
         - rating (int): Rating score (1-5)
         - useful (int): Useful votes received
         - funny (int): Funny votes received
         - cool (int): Cool votes received
         - text (str): Review text content
         - date (str): Review date

     - tip
       - This table contains short user tips about Yelp businesses, usually concise suggestions or comments, along with optional compliments and timestamps.
       - Fields:
         - user_id (str or null): User identifier
         - business_ref (str): Business identifier linking to the business collection
         - text (str): Tip text content
         - date (str): Tip date
         - compliment_count (int): Number of compliments received

     - user
       - This table contains user profile information from Yelp, including activity statistics and membership history.
       - Fields:
         - user_id (str): Unique user identifier
         - name (str): User name
         - review_count (int): Total reviews written by user
         - yelping_since (str): Registration date
         - useful (int): Total useful votes received
         - funny (int): Total funny votes received
         - cool (int): Total cool votes received
         - elite (str): Elite status years

HINTS:
- The "business_id" field in the business collection corresponds to the "business_ref" fields in both the review table and the tip table. The values differ only by their prefixes: "business_id" uses the prefix `businessid_`, while "business_ref" uses the prefix `businessref_`. For example, `businessid_1` in the business collection corresponds to `businessref_1` in the review and tip tables.
- The datasets contain five tables/collections in total. Carefully identify which tables or collections and which fields contain the information required to answer the query.
- The business collection's "description" field includes location information if needed.
- The business collection's "attributes" field includes services information if needed.

[{"review_id": "reviewid_135", "user_id": "userid_548", "business_ref": "businessref_34", "rating": "2", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.", "date": "August 01, 2016 at 03:44 AM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1067", "user_id": "userid_213", "business_ref": "businessref_89", "rating": "5", "useful": "2", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too", "date": "June 14, 2021 at 11:39 AM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_871", "user_id": "userid_616", "business_ref": "businessref_82", "rating": "4", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this \"burger\" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our \"server\" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!", "date": "29 May 2013, 23:01"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_314", "user_id": "userid_1903", "business_ref": "businessref_66", "rating": "2", "useful": "1", "funny": "2", "cool": "1", "text": "This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare", "date": "21 May 2016, 18:48"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_487", "user_id": "None", "business_ref": "businessref_95", "rating": "1", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.", "date": "2013-07-08 21:47:00"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_441", "user_id": "userid_380", "business_ref": "businessref_40", "rating": "4", "useful": "0", "funny": "1", "cool": "0", "text": "You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.", "date": "March 02, 2009 at 09:43 PM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_40", "user_id": "None", "business_ref": "businessref_47", "rating": "5", "useful": "1", "funny": "0", "cool": "1", "text": "Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.", "date": "March 16, 2014 at 12:40 AM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1807", "user_id": "userid_1546", "business_ref": "businessref_16", "rating": "3", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.", "date": "September 18, 2014 at 05:50 PM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1741", "user_id": "userid_1348", "business_ref": "businessref_96", "rating": "5", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.", "date": "17 Apr 2017, 14:04"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_418", "user_id": "userid_546", "business_ref": "businessref_46", "rating": "5", "useful": "1", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "This is some quality BBQ! I found the food to be excellent and the staff to be kind/helpful. The brisket is so tasty, and the ribs were fall-off-the-bone good! The restaurant also uses this phone app called GiftAMeal, which let me donate 2 free meals to a food pantry just by snapping a pic of my food via the app. I thought that was really neat, on top of a great dining experience. I shall return.", "date": "2016-08-15 21:16:00"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1222", "user_id": "userid_254", "business_ref": "businessref_21", "rating": "1", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "I really wish i had checked out the Yelp reviews beforehand.  I've purchased about 10 used cars in the last 25 years. This has been the worst experience by far. Test drove and purchased a used car on 3/31 for my 16 year old daughter, the salesman knew it was for her. Bought the 3 year 'bumper to bumper' warranty the finance guy said was the same as if it \"just came off the showroom floor\". They needed to remove pin stripes and fix a tire pressure sensor so they agreed to deliver it; we live almost two hours away. Met them after dark near the highway on 4/2; the check engine light came on on our way home. Texted a pic to the salesman and called him the next day -- no response. Left several voice mails for the warranty/finance guy - no response. Took it to a friend at local dealer who hooked it up to the diagnostic machine and said it was the after-market high-flow air intake that caused the warning light. I would not have purchased the car had I known that. Then another warning light - reduced engine performance, reduce clutch use. To make a very long story short - clutch goes, daughter gets stranded on busy highway, vehicle towed back to Ford of Port Richey; lots of promises about fixing it and being at the front of the service line, zero follow up, 53 days of ownership, 31 days out of commission/in shop and the story ends with a bill for $2000, even though they ultimately acknowledged one of their managers drove the car for three days because the check engine light kept coming on before they sold it. I won't even mention the cracked windshield we saw the morning after they delivered - just turn it in on your insurance. I understand it is free but still counts as a claim.   I'm just glad 16 year old girls are patient and understanding.", "date": "08 Jun 2019, 07:10"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1928", "user_id": "userid_1151", "business_ref": "businessref_9", "rating": "5", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "I had a great experience at Coffee House Too. I came on a Sunday around brunch and there was no wait for a table, and the service was exceptional. As you wait for your food, you are offered a complimentary mimosa as well as a muffin. On top of that, the cost for food is relatively low compared to other brunch locations. I highly recommend this restaurant and will definitely be coming back!", "date": "2016-07-18 21:37:00"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1790", "user_id": "userid_668", "business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "1", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Breakfast is very expensive at $7 for a breakfast sandwich meal with oj. You could get the same deal at a sit down restaurant. The oj was warm so I asked for ice. The woman at the window informed me that if I wanted ice, I had to ask for it when I placed the order. At that time, I didn't know the oj was warm. I don't ever go to McDonald's but, if do again, I would go to the one right down the street near

function.arguments:
{"code": "import json
import re
from collections import defaultdict

# Get all businesses that accept credit cards
credit_card_businesses = []
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:34']

print('Total businesses with credit cards:', len(businesses))

# Extract categories from business descriptions
category_counts = defaultdict(int)
business_ratings = defaultdict(list)

# Process each business that accepts credit cards
for business in businesses:
    business_id = business['business_id']
    description = business.get('description', '')
    
    # Extract categories from description (look for phrases after "in" or categories separated by commas)
    categories = []
    
    # Look for "in Education, Elementary Schools, ..." pattern
    if 'services in' in description:
        parts = description.split('services in')
        if len(parts) > 1:
            category_part = parts[1]
            # Remove location info if present
            if ', this' in category_part:
                category_part = category_part.split(', this')[0]
            # Split by commas and clean
            cats = [cat.strip() for cat in category_part.split(',')]
            categories.extend(cats)
    
    # Also look for "including Hair Salons, Beauty & Spas, ..." pattern
    if 'including' in description:
        parts = description.split('including')
        if len(parts) > 1:
            category_part = parts[1]
            if '.' in category_part:
                category_part = category_part.split('.')[0]
            cats = [cat.strip() for cat in category_part.split(',')]
            categories.extend(cats)
    
    # Clean up categories
    categories = [cat for cat in categories if cat and len(cat) > 2]
    
    if not categories:
        # If no clear categories found, use a generic one
        categories = ['General Business']
    
    print(f"Business: {business['name']}")
    print(f"  Categories: {categories}")
    
    # Store business ID for later rating lookup
    for cat in categories:
        category_counts[cat] += 1
        
print('\nCategory counts:', dict(category_counts))

# Now let's get ratings for these businesses from the review table
print('\nLooking up ratings...')"}

function.arguments:
{"code": "import json
import re
import os
from collections import defaultdict

# Step 1: Get businesses that accept credit cards
credit_card_key = 'var_functions.query_db:52'
businesses = locals()[credit_card_key]

print('Total businesses accepting credit cards:', len(businesses))

# Step 2: Extract categories from descriptions
category_counts = defaultdict(int)
business_to_categories = {}

for business in businesses:
    business_id = business['business_id']
    description = business.get('description', '')
    
    # Extract categories - look for patterns like "services in X, Y, Z" or "including X, Y, Z"
    categories = []
    
    # Pattern 1: "services in Education, Elementary Schools, ..."
    if 'services in' in description:
        match = re.search(r'services in (.+?)(?:\.|, this)', description)
        if match:
            cats = [c.strip() for c in match.group(1).split(',') if c.strip()]
            categories.extend(cats)
    
    # Pattern 2: "including Hair Salons, Beauty & Spas, ..." 
    if 'including' in description:
        match = re.search(r'including (.+?)(?:\.| located at)', description, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            cats = [c.strip() for c in match.group(1).split(',') if c.strip()]
            categories.extend(cats)
    
    # Clean categories - remove "and" from the last item
    cleaned_categories = []
    for cat in categories:
        cat = cat.strip()
        if cat.startswith('and '):
            cat = cat[4:]
        if cat:
            cleaned_categories.append(cat)
    
    if not cleaned_categories:
        cleaned_categories = ['General Business']
    
    business_to_categories[business_id] = cleaned_categories
    for cat in cleaned_categories:
        category_counts[cat] += 1

# Find the category with most businesses
top_category = max(category_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1])
print('\nTop category:', top_category)

# Get all business IDs in the top category
top_category_name = top_category[0]
businesses_in_top_category = []
for business_id, categories in business_to_categories.items():
    if top_category_name in categories:
        businesses_in_top_category.append(business_id)

print('Businesses in top category:', len(businesses_in_top_category))
print('Business IDs:', businesses_in_top_category)

# Save results for next steps
result = {
    'top_category': top_category_name,
    'business_count': top_category[1],
    'business_ids': businesses_in_top_category
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))"}

message.reasoning:
I need to analyze the data to find which business category has the most businesses accepting credit cards and calculate the average rating for businesses in that category.

Let me carefully plan this:

1. First, I need to get all businesses that accept credit cards from MongoDB
2. Then extract categories from the business descriptions
3. Count businesses per category
4. Get reviews from DuckDB for businesses in the top category
5. Calculate average rating for that category

From the data I've seen, business descriptions contain category information like:
- "Education, Elementary Schools, Child Care & Day Care, Local Services, Preschools, and Montessori Schools"
- "Hair Salons, Beauty & Spas, Hair Stylists, Skin Care, Blow Dry/Out Services, and Makeup Artists"

I should extract these categories and count them.

Then I need to:
1. Map business_id to business_ref (change prefix from businessid_ to businessref_)
2. Look up reviews for those businesses
3. Calculate average rating

Let me write the proper Python code now.

You are a data analysis agent. Use only the tools listed below to answer the user's query, based on the provided DATABASE DESCRIPTION for logical database names and their types (SQL or MongoDB), and the results of previous tool calls.

TOOLS (system will execute):
- query_db: run a SQL or Mongo query. Returns a list of JSON-serializable records or an error string.
  Required args: {"db_name": "<logical_db_name>", "query": "<SQL or Mongo query>"}
- list_db: list tables or collections for a given database.
  Required args: {"db_name": "<logical_db_name>"}
- execute_python: run Python code.
  Required args: {"code": "<python_code>"}
- return_answer: finish and return the final answer (plain text).
  Required args: {"answer": "<final plain-text answer>"}

INSTRUCTIONS:
1. After each tool call, its result will be stored in a storage under a key named after the tool call id (you will be told the key name). The next message will include both the result (or a preview if it's large) and the storage key name.
2. Inside execute_python code you may read storage entries using the provided key names, e.g., if the tool call id is 'functions.tool:id', you can access its result via `locals()['var_functions.tool:id']` in your code.
3. You cannot modify or reassign those storage-provided variables; you may read them and create new variables as needed.
4. If a tool result is large, the next message will include a preview (first 10000 characters) and the storage entry will be the .json file path (a string) where the full result is stored. To access the full result, your execute_python code must open and read that .json file.

KEY RULES (must follow exactly):
1. Always use tool calls. Do not output plain text, explanations, or reasoning.
2. Include all required arguments for the tool you call.
3. For query_db, always specify db_name and query. Refer the DATABASE DESCRIPTION for db_name and query format.
4. For PostgreSQL, wrap mixed-case or uppercase column names in double quotes.
5. For list_db, refer the DATABASE DESCRIPTION to specify db_name.
6. Use execute_python for data processing as needed. 
7. When using execute_python, your code will be quoted by triple double-quotes and passed as a string to `exec(...)` for execution in a Python 3.12 environment with only pandas and pyarrow installed. So you must ensure your code is compatible with this execution method. For exampe, do not use triple double-quotes in your code, as they may interfere with parsing. Do not use non-built-in or non-installed packages.
8. When using execute_python, your code must print the result at the end exactly as shown in the PRINT FORMAT section below. The printed result must be a string that can be successfully parsed by json.loads() without errors.

PRINT FORMAT (must match exactly):
----BEGIN PRINT FORMAT----
print("__RESULT__:")
print(your_json_serializable_string_here)
----END PRINT FORMAT----

For simple types (int, float, str, bool, None), you may use json.dumps() to produce a valid JSON string.
For complex or non-JSON-serializable types, you must convert them into JSON-compatible forms before printing.
For lists or dictionaries, you must ensure that all nested elements are also converted into JSON-serializable types.
9. Return the final answer only via a single return_answer tool call. Do not include extra text, explanation, or formatting.

EXAMPLES:
- query_db:
{"tool": "query_db", "args": {"db_name": "some_db_name", "query": "SELECT * FROM some_table LIMIT some_limit;"}} # for SQL databases
{"tool": "query_db", "args": {"db_name": "some_db_name", "query": "{"collection": "some_collection", "filter": {some_filter}, "projection": {some_projection}, "limit": some_limit}"}} # for MongoDB databases

- list_db:
{"tool": "list_db", "args": {"db_name": "some_db_name"}}

- execute_python:
{"tool": "execute_python", "args": {"code": "import pandas as pd
# rl1 and rl2 are the keys of two JSON-serializable record lists in storage
df1 = pd.DataFrame(rl1)
df2 = pd.DataFrame(rl2)
result = pd.merge(df1, df2, on='id').head(10).to_json(orient='records')
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)"}}

- return_answer:
{"tool": "return_answer", "args": {"answer": "…final plain-text answer…"}}

If you cannot proceed, call return_answer with a short explanatory message.

Do not output explanations, reasoning, or any natural language outside of the required tool calls.

QUERY:
Which business category has the largest number of businesses that accept credit card payments, and what is its average rating?

DATABASE DESCRIPTION:
You are working with two databases to solve this query.

Here are the descriptions of these two databases:

1. businessinfo_database
   - This database is stored in a MongoDB database and contains metadata and user check-in records for Yelp businesses. It captures business attributes, service descriptions, operating hours, and user visit timestamps.
   - This database consists of two collections:
     - business
       - This collection contains metadata for businesses listed on Yelp, including names, identifiers, operational status, attributes, business hours, and service/location descriptions.
       - Fields:
         - _id
         - business_id (str): Unique business identifier
         - name (str): Business name
         - review_count (int): Total number of reviews
         - is_open (int): Whether business is currently open (1=open, 0=closed)
         - attributes (dict or null): Business attributes (parking, WiFi, etc.)
         - hours (dict or null): Operating hours
         - description (str): Business description including location information

     - checkin
       - This collection records the check-in behavior of Yelp users at various businesses, capturing the timestamped log of user presence.
       - Fields:
         - _id
         - business_id (str): Business identifier linking to business collection
         - date (list of str): List of check-in timestamps

2. user_database
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains user profiles and user-generated content from Yelp, including written reviews, tips, and engagement statistics such as review counts, votes, and elite status.
   - This database consists of three tables:
     - review
       - This table contains user-generated reviews of businesses on Yelp, including rating scores, review content, and metadata such as user and business reference identifiers.
       - Fields:
         - review_id (str): Unique review identifier
         - user_id (str or null): User identifier
         - business_ref (str): Business identifier linking to the business collection
         - rating (int): Rating score (1-5)
         - useful (int): Useful votes received
         - funny (int): Funny votes received
         - cool (int): Cool votes received
         - text (str): Review text content
         - date (str): Review date

     - tip
       - This table contains short user tips about Yelp businesses, usually concise suggestions or comments, along with optional compliments and timestamps.
       - Fields:
         - user_id (str or null): User identifier
         - business_ref (str): Business identifier linking to the business collection
         - text (str): Tip text content
         - date (str): Tip date
         - compliment_count (int): Number of compliments received

     - user
       - This table contains user profile information from Yelp, including activity statistics and membership history.
       - Fields:
         - user_id (str): Unique user identifier
         - name (str): User name
         - review_count (int): Total reviews written by user
         - yelping_since (str): Registration date
         - useful (int): Total useful votes received
         - funny (int): Total funny votes received
         - cool (int): Total cool votes received
         - elite (str): Elite status years

HINTS:
- The "business_id" field in the business collection corresponds to the "business_ref" fields in both the review table and the tip table. The values differ only by their prefixes: "business_id" uses the prefix `businessid_`, while "business_ref" uses the prefix `businessref_`. For example, `businessid_1` in the business collection corresponds to `businessref_1` in the review and tip tables.
- The datasets contain five tables/collections in total. Carefully identify which tables or collections and which fields contain the information required to answer the query.
- The business collection's "description" field includes location information if needed.
- The business collection's "attributes" field includes services information if needed.

[{"review_id": "reviewid_135", "user_id": "userid_548", "business_ref": "businessref_34", "rating": "2", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.", "date": "August 01, 2016 at 03:44 AM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1067", "user_id": "userid_213", "business_ref": "businessref_89", "rating": "5", "useful": "2", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too", "date": "June 14, 2021 at 11:39 AM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_871", "user_id": "userid_616", "business_ref": "businessref_82", "rating": "4", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this \"burger\" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our \"server\" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!", "date": "29 May 2013, 23:01"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_314", "user_id": "userid_1903", "business_ref": "businessref_66", "rating": "2", "useful": "1", "funny": "2", "cool": "1", "text": "This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare", "date": "21 May 2016, 18:48"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_487", "user_id": "None", "business_ref": "businessref_95", "rating": "1", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened", "date": "November 01, 2021 at 05:11 PM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1031", "user_id": "userid_206", "business_ref": "businessref_24", "rating": "4", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "A cornucupia of flavors and toppings. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.", "date": "2013-07-08 21:47:00"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_441", "user_id": "userid_380", "business_ref": "businessref_40", "rating": "4", "useful": "0", "funny": "1", "cool": "0", "text": "You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.", "date": "March 02, 2009 at 09:43 PM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_40", "user_id": "None", "business_ref": "businessref_47", "rating": "5", "useful": "1", "funny": "0", "cool": "1", "text": "Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.", "date": "March 16, 2014 at 12:40 AM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1807", "user_id": "userid_1546", "business_ref": "businessref_16", "rating": "3", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.", "date": "September 18, 2014 at 05:50 PM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1741", "user_id": "userid_1348", "business_ref": "businessref_96", "rating": "5", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.", "date": "17 Apr 2017, 14:04"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_418", "user_id": "userid_546", "business_ref": "businessref_46", "rating": "5", "useful": "1", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "This is some quality BBQ! I found the food to be excellent and the staff to be kind/helpful. The brisket is so tasty, and the ribs were fall-off-the-bone good! The restaurant also uses this phone app called GiftAMeal, which let me donate 2 free meals to a food pantry just by snapping a pic of my food via the app. I thought that was really neat, on top of a great dining experience. I shall return.", "date": "2016-08-15 21:16:00"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1222", "user_id": "userid_254", "business_ref": "businessref_21", "rating": "1", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "I really wish i had checked out the Yelp reviews beforehand.  I've purchased about 10 used cars in the last 25 years. This has been the worst experience by far. Test drove and purchased a used car on 3/31 for my 16 year old daughter, the salesman knew it was for her. Bought the 3 year 'bumper to bumper' warranty the finance guy said was the same as if it \"just came off the showroom floor\". They needed to remove pin stripes and fix a tire pressure sensor so they agreed to deliver it; we live almost two hours away. Met them after dark near the highway on 4/2; the check engine light came on on our way home. Texted a pic to the salesman and called him the next day -- no response. Left several voice mails for the warranty/finance guy - no response. Took it to a friend at local dealer who hooked it up to the diagnostic machine and said it was the after-market high-flow air intake that caused the warning light. I would not have purchased the car had I known that. Then another warning light - reduced engine performance, reduce clutch use. To make a very long story short - clutch goes, daughter gets stranded on busy highway, vehicle towed back to Ford of Port Richey; lots of promises about fixing it and being at the front of the service line, zero follow up, 53 days of ownership, 31 days out of commission/in shop and the story ends with a bill for $2000, even though they ultimately acknowledged one of their managers drove the car for three days because the check engine light kept coming on before they sold it. I won't even mention the cracked windshield we saw the morning after they delivered - just turn it in on your insurance. I understand it is free but still counts as a claim.   I'm just glad 16 year old girls are patient and understanding.", "date": "08 Jun 2019, 07:10"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1928", "user_id": "userid_1151", "business_ref": "businessref_9", "rating": "5", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "I had a great experience at Coffee House Too. I came on a Sunday around brunch and there was no wait for a table, and the service was exceptional. As you wait for your food, you are offered a complimentary mimosa as well as a muffin. On top of that, the cost for food is relatively low compared to other brunch locations. I highly recommend this restaurant and will definitely be coming back!", "date": "2016-07-18 21:37:00"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1790", "user_id": "userid_668", "business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "1", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Breakfast is very expensive at $7 for a breakfast sandwich meal with oj. You could get the same deal at a sit down restaurant. The oj was warm so I asked for ice. The woman at the window informed me that if I wanted ice, I had to ask for it when I placed the order. At that time, I didn't know the oj was warm. I don't ever go to McDonald's but, if do again, I would go to the one right down the street near

{"code": "import json
import re
from collections import defaultdict

# Get all businesses that accept credit cards
credit_card_businesses = []
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:34']

print('Total businesses with credit cards:', len(businesses))

# Extract categories from business descriptions
category_counts = defaultdict(int)
business_ratings = defaultdict(list)

# Process each business that accepts credit cards
for business in businesses:
    business_id = business['business_id']
    description = business.get('description', '')
    
    # Extract categories from description (look for phrases after "in" or categories separated by commas)
    categories = []
    
    # Look for "in Education, Elementary Schools, ..." pattern
    if 'services in' in description:
        parts = description.split('services in')
        if len(parts) > 1:
            category_part = parts[1]
            # Remove location info if present
            if ', this' in category_part:
                category_part = category_part.split(', this')[0]
            # Split by commas and clean
            cats = [cat.strip() for cat in category_part.split(',')]
            categories.extend(cats)
    
    # Also look for "including Hair Salons, Beauty & Spas, ..." pattern
    if 'including' in description:
        parts = description.split('including')
        if len(parts) > 1:
            category_part = parts[1]
            if '.' in category_part:
                category_part = category_part.split('.')[0]
            cats = [cat.strip() for cat in category_part.split(',')]
            categories.extend(cats)
    
    # Clean up categories
    categories = [cat for cat in categories if cat and len(cat) > 2]
    
    if not categories:
        # If no clear categories found, use a generic one
        categories = ['General Business']
    
    print(f"Business: {business['name']}")
    print(f"  Categories: {categories}")
    
    # Store business ID for later rating lookup
    for cat in categories:
        category_counts[cat] += 1
        
print('\nCategory counts:', dict(category_counts))

# Now let's get ratings for these businesses from the review table
print('\nLooking up ratings...')"}

function.arguments:
{"code": "import json, re, os
from collections import defaultdict

# Read business data
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:34']
print('Total credit card businesses:', len(businesses))

# Extract categories and map business IDs to categories
category_counts = defaultdict(int)
business_to_categories = {}

for business in businesses:
    business_id = business['business_id']
    desc = business.get('description', '')
    name = business.get('name', '')
    
    # Find categories in description
    categories = []
    if 'services in' in desc:
        part = desc.split('services in')[-1]
        if ', this' in part:
            part = part.split(', this')[0]
        cats = [c.strip() for c in part.split(',') if c.strip()]
        categories.extend(cats)
    
    if 'including' in desc:
        part = desc.split('including')[-1]
        if '.' in part:
            part = part.split('.')[0]
        cats = [c.strip() for c in part.split(',') if c.strip()]
        categories.extend(cats)
    
    if not categories:
        categories = ['General Business']
    
    business_to_categories[business_id] = categories
    for cat in categories:
        category_counts[cat] += 1

print('Top categories:', sorted(category_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:10])

# Read review data
review_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:24']
if isinstance(review_file, str) and os.path.exists(review_file):
    with open(review_file, 'r') as f:
        reviews = json.load(f)
    print('Total reviews:', len(reviews))
else:
    reviews = []
    print('No review file found')"}

message.reasoning:
The user is asking about which business category has the largest number of businesses that accept credit card payments, and what is its average rating.

I need to:
1. Find all businesses that accept credit card payments (from business collection)
2. Categorize these businesses based on their descriptions
3. Count businesses per category
4. Find the top category with most businesses
5. Get the ratings for businesses in that category (from review table)
6. Calculate the average rating

Let me start by understanding the data structure better. From the queries I've run:
- Business data is in MongoDB (businessinfo_database, business collection)
- Review data is in DuckDB (user_database, review table)
- business_id in business collection corresponds to business_ref in review table (with prefix differences: businessid_ vs businessref_)

Let me fix the Python code and properly extract categories and ratings. The issue is with string quoting.

messages.content:
You are a data analysis agent. Use only the tools listed below to answer the user's query, based on the provided DATABASE DESCRIPTION for logical database names and their types (SQL or MongoDB), and the results of previous tool calls.

TOOLS (system will execute):
- query_db: run a SQL or Mongo query. Returns a list of JSON-serializable records or an error string.
  Required args: {"db_name": "<logical_db_name>", "query": "<SQL or Mongo query>"}
- list_db: list tables or collections for a given database.
  Required args: {"db_name": "<logical_db_name>"}
- execute_python: run Python code.
  Required args: {"code": "<python_code>"}
- return_answer: finish and return the final answer (plain text).
  Required args: {"answer": "<final plain-text answer>"}

INSTRUCTIONS:
1. After each tool call, its result will be stored in a storage under a key named after the tool call id (you will be told the key name). The next message will include both the result (or a preview if it's large) and the storage key name.
2. Inside execute_python code you may read storage entries using the provided key names, e.g., if the tool call id is 'functions.tool:id', you can access its result via `locals()['var_functions.tool:id']` in your code.
3. You cannot modify or reassign those storage-provided variables; you may read them and create new variables as needed.
4. If a tool result is large, the next message will include a preview (first 10000 characters) and the storage entry will be the .json file path (a string) where the full result is stored. To access the full result, your execute_python code must open and read that .json file.

KEY RULES (must follow exactly):
1. Always use tool calls. Do not output plain text, explanations, or reasoning.
2. Include all required arguments for the tool you call.
3. For query_db, always specify db_name and query. Refer the DATABASE DESCRIPTION for db_name and query format.
4. For PostgreSQL, wrap mixed-case or uppercase column names in double quotes.
5. For list_db, refer the DATABASE DESCRIPTION to specify db_name.
6. Use execute_python for data processing as needed. 
7. When using execute_python, your code will be quoted by triple double-quotes and passed as a string to `exec(...)` for execution in a Python 3.12 environment with only pandas and pyarrow installed. So you must ensure your code is compatible with this execution method. For exampe, do not use triple double-quotes in your code, as they may interfere with parsing. Do not use non-built-in or non-installed packages.
8. When using execute_python, your code must print the result at the end exactly as shown in the PRINT FORMAT section below. The printed result must be a string that can be successfully parsed by json.loads() without errors.

PRINT FORMAT (must match exactly):
----BEGIN PRINT FORMAT----
print("__RESULT__:")
print(your_json_serializable_string_here)
----END PRINT FORMAT----

For simple types (int, float, str, bool, None), you may use json.dumps() to produce a valid JSON string.
For complex or non-JSON-serializable types, you must convert them into JSON-compatible forms before printing.
For lists or dictionaries, you must ensure that all nested elements are also converted into JSON-serializable types.
9. Return the final answer only via a single return_answer tool call. Do not include extra text, explanation, or formatting.

- query_db:
{"tool": "query_db", "args": {"db_name": "some_db_name", "query": "SELECT * FROM some_table LIMIT some_limit;"}} # for SQL databases
{"tool": "query_db", "args": {"db_name": "some_db_name", "query": "{"collection": "some_collection", "filter": {some_filter}, "projection": {some_projection}, "limit": some_limit}"}} # for MongoDB databases

QUERY:
Which business category has the largest number of businesses that accept credit card payments, and what is its average rating?

DATABASE DESCRIPTION:
You are working with two databases to solve this query.

Here are the descriptions of these two databases:

1. businessinfo_database
   - This database is stored in a MongoDB database and contains metadata and user check-in records for Yelp businesses. It captures business attributes, service descriptions, operating hours, and user visit timestamps.
   - This database consists of two collections:
     - business
       - This collection contains metadata for businesses listed on Yelp, including names, identifiers, operational status, attributes, business hours, and service/location descriptions.
       - Fields:
         - _id
         - business_id (str): Unique business identifier
         - name (str): Business name
         - review_count (int): Total number of reviews
         - is_open (int): Whether business is currently open (1=open, 0=closed)
         - attributes (dict or null): Business attributes (parking, WiFi, etc.)
         - hours (dict or null): Operating hours
         - description (str): Business description including location information

     - checkin
       - This collection records the check-in behavior of Yelp users at various businesses, capturing the timestamped log of user presence.
       - Fields:
         - _id
         - business_id (str): Business identifier linking to business collection
         - date (list of str): List of check-in timestamps

2. user_database
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains user profiles and user-generated content from Yelp, including written reviews, tips, and engagement statistics such as review counts, votes, and elite status.
   - This database consists of three tables:
     - review
       - This table contains user-generated reviews of businesses on Yelp, including rating scores, review content, and metadata such as user and business reference identifiers.
       - Fields:
         - review_id (str): Unique review identifier
         - user_id (str or null): User identifier
         - business_ref (str): Business identifier linking to the business collection
         - rating (int): Rating score (1-5)
         - useful (int): Useful votes received
         - funny (int): Funny votes received
         - cool (int): Cool votes received
         - text (str): Review text content
         - date (str): Review date

     - tip
       - This table contains short user tips about Yelp businesses, usually concise suggestions or comments, along with optional compliments and timestamps.
       - Fields:
         - user_id (str or null): User identifier
         - business_ref (str): Business identifier linking to the business collection
         - text (str): Tip text content
         - date (str): Tip date
         - compliment_count (int): Number of compliments received

     - user
       - This table contains user profile information from Yelp, including activity statistics and membership history.
       - Fields:
         - user_id (str): Unique user identifier
         - name (str): User name
         - review_count (int): Total reviews written by user
         - yelping_since (str): Registration date
         - useful (int): Total useful votes received
         - funny (int): Total funny votes received
         - cool (int): Total cool votes received
         - elite (str): Elite status years

HINTS:
- The "business_id" field in the business collection corresponds to the "business_ref" fields in both the review table and the tip table. The values differ only by their prefixes: "business_id" uses the prefix `businessid_`, while "business_ref" uses the prefix `businessref_`. For example, `businessid_1` in the business collection corresponds to `businessref_1` in the review and tip tables.
- The datasets contain five tables/collections in total. Carefully identify which tables or collections and which fields contain the information required to answer the query.
- The business collection's "description" field includes location information if needed.
- The business collection's "attributes" field includes services information if needed.

Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.", "date": "March 02, 2009 at 09:43 PM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_40", "user_id": "None", "business_ref": "businessref_47", "rating": "5", "useful": "1", "funny": "0", "cool": "1", "text": "Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.", "date": "September 18, 2014 at 05:50 PM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1741", "user_id": "userid_1348", "business_ref": "businessref_96", "rating": "5", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.", "date": "17 Apr 2017, 14:04"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_418", "user_id": "userid_546", "business_ref": "businessref_46", "rating": "5", "useful": "1", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "This is some quality BBQ! I found the food to be excellent and the staff to be kind/helpful. The brisket is so tasty, and the ribs were fall-off-the-bone good! The restaurant also uses this phone app called GiftAMeal, which let me donate 2 free meals to a food pantry just by snapping a pic of my food via the app. I thought that was really neat, on top of a great dining experience. I shall return.", "date": "2016-08-15 21:16:00"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1222", "user_id": "userid_254", "business_ref": "businessref_21", "rating": "1", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "I really wish i had checked out the Yelp reviews beforehand.  I've purchased about 10 used cars in the last 25 years. This has been the worst experience by far. Test drove and purchased a used car on 3/31 for my 16 year old daughter, the salesman knew it was for her. Bought the 3 year 'bumper to bumper' warranty the finance guy said was the same as if it \"just came off the showroom floor\". They needed to remove pin stripes and fix a tire pressure sensor so they agreed to deliver it; we live almost two hours away. Met them after dark near the highway on 4/2; the check engine light came on on our way home. Texted a pic to the salesman and called him the next day -- no response. Left several voice mails for the warranty/finance guy - no response. Took it to a friend at local dealer who hooked it up to the diagnostic machine and said it was the after-market high-flow air intake that caused the warning light. I would not have purchased the car had I known that. Then another warning light - reduced engine performance, reduce clutch use. To make a very long story short - clutch goes, daughter gets stranded on busy highway, vehicle towed back to Ford of Port Richey; lots of promises about fixing it and being at the front of the service line, zero follow up, 53 days of ownership, 31 days out of commission/in shop and the story ends with a bill for $2000, even though they ultimately acknowledged one of their managers drove the car for three days because the check engine light kept coming on before they sold it. I won't even mention the cracked windshield we saw the morning after they delivered - just turn it in on your insurance. I understand it is free but still counts as a claim.   I'm just glad 16 year old girls are patient and understanding.", "date": "08 Jun 2019, 07:10"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1928", "user_id": "userid_1151", "business_ref": "businessref_9", "rating": "5", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "I had a great experience at Coffee House Too. I came on a Sunday around brunch and there was no wait for a table, and the service was exceptional. As you wait for your food, you are offered a complimentary mimosa as well as a muffin. On top of that, the cost for food is relatively low compared to other brunch locations. I highly recommend this restaurant and will definitely be coming back!", "date": "2016-07-18 21:37:00"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1790", "user_id": "userid_668", "business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "1", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Breakfast is very expensive at $7 for a breakfast sandwich meal with oj. At that time, I didn't know the oj was warm. I don't ever go to McDonald's but, if do again, I would go to the one right down the street near

function.arguments:
{"code": "import json
import re
import os
from collections import defaultdict

# Step 1: Get businesses that accept credit cards
credit_card_key = 'var_functions.query_db:52'
businesses = locals()[credit_card_key]

print('Total businesses accepting credit cards:', len(businesses))

# Step 2: Extract categories from descriptions
category_counts = defaultdict(int)
business_to_categories = {}

for business in businesses:
    business_id = business['business_id']
    description = business.get('description', '')
    
    # Extract categories - look for patterns like "services in X, Y, Z" or "including X, Y, Z"
    categories = []
    
    # Pattern 1: "services in Education, Elementary Schools, ..."
    if 'services in' in description:
        match = re.search(r'services in (.+?)(?:\.|, this)', description)
        if match:
            cats = [c.strip() for c in match.group(1).split(',') if c.strip()]
            categories.extend(cats)
    
    # Pattern 2: "including Hair Salons, Beauty & Spas, ..." 
    if 'including' in description:
        match = re.search(r'including (.+?)(?:\.| located at)', description, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            cats = [c.strip() for c in match.group(1).split(',') if c.strip()]
            categories.extend(cats)
    
    # Clean categories - remove "and" from the last item
    cleaned_categories = []
    for cat in categories:
        cat = cat.strip()
        if cat.startswith('and '):
            cat = cat[4:]
        if cat:
            cleaned_categories.append(cat)
    
    if not cleaned_categories:
        cleaned_categories = ['General Business']
    
    business_to_categories[business_id] = cleaned_categories
    for cat in cleaned_categories:
        category_counts[cat] += 1

# Find the category with most businesses
top_category = max(category_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1])
print('\nTop category:', top_category)

# Get all business IDs in the top category
top_category_name = top_category[0]
businesses_in_top_category = []
for business_id, categories in business_to_categories.items():
    if top_category_name in categories:
        businesses_in_top_category.append(business_id)

print('Businesses in top category:', len(businesses_in_top_category))
print('Business IDs:', businesses_in_top_category)

# Save results for next steps
result = {
    'top_category': top_category_name,
    'business_count': top_category[1],
    'business_ids': businesses_in_top_category
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))"}

If you cannot proceed, call return_answer with a short explanatory message.
